title: amphymenium rohrii
intro: Amphymenium rohrii, commonly known as Rohri's liverwort, is a species of liverwort that belongs to the family Lepidoziaceae. It is a small, non-vascular plant that grows in tropical and subtropical regions, often in shaded and humid areas. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Amphymenium rohrii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions, including its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties.

side_effects: Amphymenium rohrii can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing nausea, dizziness, and headaches in some individuals due to its high concentration of toxic compounds.
